description:
A compliance analyst has asked you to set-up a **very small “one-touch” audit automation** workspace.

    1. The working directory is **/home/user/compliance_audit/**
       • This directory already contains a sub-directory named **sample_data/** with a few exemplar files.  
       • Your job is to add a **Makefile** that lets the analyst type one of three short commands in order to
         a) list the files being audited,  
         b) calculate their SHA-256 checksums, and  
         c) generate a final pipe-delimited audit trail.

    2. REQUIRED MAKE TARGETS  
       • **list**       writes a file called **/home/user/compliance_audit/file_list.log** containing nothing except the relative paths of every regular file under *sample_data/*, one path per line, sorted in byte-collated order.  
       • **checksum**   writes a file called **/home/user/compliance_audit/checksum.log** whose contents are the two fields  
            SHA256 hex digest  + “<space>” + *relative file path*  
          (i.e. identical to coreutils’ `sha256sum` output) for every file in *file_list.log*, also sorted.  
       • **audit**      depends on the first two targets and produces **/home/user/compliance_audit/audit.log** with the *exact* format  

            <relative_path>|<SHA256 hex digest>

         Every line in *audit.log* **MUST** match the order of *file_list.log*. No extra
         whitespace before/after any field or at line ends.

    3. TARGET RELATIONSHIPS  
       • Running `make audit` should automatically run the other two targets if their
         respective output files are missing or older than their prerequisites.  
       • `make list` and `make checksum` can be executed independently.

    4. OTHER REQUIREMENTS  
       • Do not hard-code the two existing sample file names; discover them programmatically so the Makefile keeps working if new files are added later.  
       • Use built-in POSIX tools only (find, sort, sha256sum, cat, etc.). No external scripting languages.  
       • The Makefile itself must live at **/home/user/compliance_audit/Makefile** and be GNU-Make compatible.  
       • When everything is complete, the analyst must be able to type the following commands from inside */home/user/compliance_audit/* and get the described results:

            make list
            make checksum
            make audit

    5. VERIFICATION CHECK
       After `make audit` has been executed once, the file  
       **/home/user/compliance_audit/audit.log** is expected to contain *exactly* the
       following two lines (including the final new-line character at the end of the second line):

            sample_data/file1.txt|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
            sample_data/file2.txt|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

       Automated tests will compare the file byte-for-byte, so make sure your Makefile
       reproduces this output exactly for the provided sample data.  
       (If additional files are later added to *sample_data/*, they of course must appear in every log in the same sorted position.)
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth set-up for automated grading.
        These items already exist when the scenario begins.

        1. Directory tree
           /home/user/compliance_audit/
           └── sample_data/
               ├── file1.txt   (empty file, size 0 bytes)
               └── file2.txt   (contains ASCII “abc” with NO trailing newline)

        2. Expected SHA-256 checksums
           sample_data/file1.txt  e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
           sample_data/file2.txt  ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

        3. The grader will:
           a) cd /home/user/compliance_audit
           b) run `make audit`
           c) assert that:
                – /home/user/compliance_audit/file_list.log  has exactly two lines:
                     sample_data/file1.txt
                     sample_data/file2.txt
                – /home/user/compliance_audit/checksum.log  has exactly two lines:
                     e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  sample_data/file1.txt
                     ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad  sample_data/file2.txt
                – /home/user/compliance_audit/audit.log  matches the two-line block shown in the public task description.
     -->
